creator: erik@tenku.dk
text:
(In reply to Nuno Teixeira from comment #42)
I'll take the challenge :-)

(In reply to Nuno Teixeira from comment #43)
Nice that the port now passes on all three amd64 builds.

It was a bit of an effort to communicate to ROOT's CMake specifications that I was on an amd64 architecture on FreeBSD, such that it would compile on my machine, when I started hacking the source code.
This is why I put amd64 as the only currently working type of architecture.

I intended to expand into e.g. arm64 further down the line, and I can have a look at i386 as well, eventually.
